object.description: This is the ChatClient class that wraps around a LLM to chat with it. It has support for adding files (images, videos, audio, pdf, text) to the chat.

descriptions.description: Adds a file by bytes to the chat message. The bytes must be a image/*, video/*, audio/*, application/pdf or text/ content type

summaries.summary: Adds a file by bytes to the chat message.

parameter.name: bytes
parameter.description: The bytes of that must be send. The content type will be detected automatically.

descriptions.description: Adds a file by bytes to the chat message. The bytes must be a image/*, video/*, audio/*, application/pdf or text/ content type

summaries.summary: Adds a file by bytes to the chat message.

parameter.name: bytes
parameter.description: The bytes of that must be send. The content type will be detected automatically.

parameter.name: contentType
parameter.description: the content the file, must be a image/*, video/*, audio/*, application/pdf or text/ content type

descriptions.description: Add a file (String or JSFile) to the chat message. The file must be a image/*, video/*, audio/*, application/pdf or text/ content type This will be guessed based on the file extension or content.

summaries.summary: Add a file (String or JSFile) to the chat message.

parameter.name: file
parameter.description: A JSFile or String (path to a file on the server)

descriptions.description: Add a file (String or JSFile) to the chat message. The file must be a image/*, video/*, audio/*, application/pdf or text/ content type

summaries.summary: Add a file (String or JSFile) to the chat message.

parameter.name: file
parameter.description: A JSFile or String (path to a file on the server)

parameter.name: contentType
parameter.description: the content the file, must be a image/*, video/*, audio/*, application/pdf or text/ content type

function.name: chat
function.return: {Promise<plugins.ai.ChatResponse>} A promise that will be resolved with the assistant response.

descriptions.description: Send a userMessage to the ai. This will return a promise that will be resolved with the response. This response is a Promise that will get a ChatResponse object in the then or the promise will be rejected with an error.

summaries.summary: Send a userMessage to the ai.

parameter.name: userMessage
parameter.description: The user message

descriptions.description: Send a userMessage to the ai. This will call the provided functions on partial response, complete response and error. So this can be used for streaming responses.

summaries.summary: Send a userMessage to the ai.

parameter.name: userMessage
parameter.description: The user message send to the ai.

parameter.name: partialResponse
parameter.description: {(partialResponse:String)=>void} A function that will be called with each partial string response from the ai.

parameter.name: onComplete
parameter.description: {(response:plugins.ai.ChatResponse)=>void} A function that will be called when the response is complete, it will be called with the ChatResponse object.

parameter.name: onError
parameter.description: {(error:Exception)=>void} A function that will be called when an error occurs.

descriptions.description:
Send a userMessage to the ai, this call is a synchronous call and will return a ChatResponse object directly. This can throw an exception of something goes wrong.
Its recommended to use the Async (Promise) version. Only use this if you directly need a respond that you need to return from a function.

summaries.summary: Send a userMessage to the ai, this call is a synchronous call and will return a ChatResponse object directly.

parameter.name: userMessage
parameter.description: The user message

descriptions.description:
Releases resources held by this chat client.
Always remember to call this when you will no longer use a chat client.

For example a client that is configured to use remote MCP Servers needs to close the connections/streams or child processes (in case of STDIO MCP transport) of the MCP Clients.

It is a good idea to have .close called inside the finally of a try-catch block, as operations after the ChatClient was built might throw exceptions - and we want resources to be released properly.

summaries.summary: Releases resources held by this chat client.

function.name: getFinishReason
function.return: the finish reason reported by the underlying response, or null if not provided

descriptions.description: Returns the reason why the model finished generating the response. For example, the finish reason could indicate it completed normally or was stopped because of reaching a token limit. See FinishReason for possible values.

summaries.summary: Returns the reason why the model finished generating the response.

function.name: getId
function.return: the response id as a String, or null if the underlying response does not provide one

descriptions.description: Returns the unique identifier for this chat response. This is typically generated by the underlying model or client library and can be used to correlate logs, trace requests, or debug conversation history.

summaries.summary: Returns the unique identifier for this chat response.

descriptions.description: Returns the original user prompt that led to this response.

summaries.summary: Returns the original user prompt that led to this response.

function.name: getResponse
function.return: the full response text (never null if constructed correctly)

descriptions.description: Returns the full response text that should be shown to the end user. This value was provided when constructing this wrapper and may include post-processed, combined or trimmed text in addition to the raw AI message.

summaries.summary: Returns the full response text that should be shown to the end user.

function.name: getThinking
function.return: the thinking text, or null if none is available

descriptions.description: Returns the 'thinking' text produced by the AI message, if any. Some models or streaming APIs include incremental "thinking" or intermediate text while composing the final answer. This method exposes that value from the underlying langchain4j AI message.

summaries.summary: Returns the 'thinking' text produced by the AI message, if any.

function.name: getTokenUsage
function.return: the token usage details reported by the underlying response, or null if not available

descriptions.description: Returns token usage information for this response. Token usage typically contains counts for prompt, completion and total tokens and can be useful for billing, monitoring or debugging token consumption.

summaries.summary: Returns token usage information for this response.

object.description: Builder like class to add a meta data column to a ServoyEmbeddingStoreBuilder.

descriptions.description: Adds the meta data column to the ServoyEmbeddingStoreBuilder and returns it.

summaries.summary: Adds the meta data column to the ServoyEmbeddingStoreBuilder and returns it.

descriptions.description: Sets the allow null option of the meta data column to add.

summaries.summary: Sets the allow null option of the meta data column to add.

parameter.name: allowNull
parameter.description: meta data column allow null option (default false).

descriptions.description: Sets the column type of the meta data column to add.

summaries.summary: Sets the column type of the meta data column to add.

parameter.name: columnType
parameter.description: meta data column type (default JSColumn.TEXT).

descriptions.description: Sets a flag for the meta data column to add, for example JSColumn.TENANT_COLUMN or JSColumn.UUID_COLUMN.

summaries.summary: Sets a flag for the meta data column to add, for example JSColumn.

parameter.name: flag
parameter.description: flag to set for the meta data column.

descriptions.description: Sets the name of the meta data column to add.

summaries.summary: Sets the name of the meta data column to add.

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: meta data column name.

object.description: EmbeddingModel provides methods to generate embeddings for text and manage embedding stores. It supports in-memory and Servoy (pgvector) embedding stores.

function.name: createInMemoryStore
function.return: An EmbeddingStore backed by an in-memory store.

descriptions.description: Creates an in-memory embedding store for storing and retrieving embeddings.

summaries.summary: Creates an in-memory embedding store for storing and retrieving embeddings.

descriptions.description: Creates a builder for servoy embedding stores.

summaries.summary: Creates a builder for servoy embedding stores.

function.name: embedding
function.return: The embedding as a float array, or null if input is empty.

descriptions.description: Generates an embedding for a single text string this can be used to use in the foundset.sort(vectorColumn, embeddingModel.embed("text"), maxRows);

summaries.summary: Generates an embedding for a single text string this can be used to use in the foundset.

parameter.name: text
parameter.description: The text string to create embeddings for.

function.name: embedding
function.return: A Promise resolving to a float array of embeddings, or null if input is empty.

descriptions.description: Generates embeddings for an array of text strings asynchronously.

summaries.summary: Generates embeddings for an array of text strings asynchronously.

parameter.name: texts
parameter.description: The array of text strings to embed.

descriptions.description: Gets the dimension of the embeddings produced by the model. This can be used when createing a vector column in a database, to use as the "size" of the vector.

summaries.summary: Gets the dimension of the embeddings produced by the model.

object.description: EmbeddingStore provides methods to embed text data and perform similarity search using embeddings. It supports asynchronous embedding and blocking search, and can be backed by various embedding store implementations.

function.name: embed
function.return: A Promise resolving to the store

descriptions.description: Embeds a PDF document by splitting it into paragraphs of specified size and overlap The text segments are stored in the embedding store and will get metadata from the pdf and if possible also the name of the PDF is set if that is known.

summaries.summary: Embeds a PDF document by splitting it into paragraphs of specified size and overlap The text segments are stored in the embedding store and will get metadata from the pdf and if possible also the name of the PDF is set if that is known.

parameter.name: pdfSource
parameter.description: This can be JSFile or String representing a file path, or byte[] representing the PDF content.

parameter.name: maxSegmentSizeInChars
parameter.description: How many characters per segment we can have.

parameter.name: maxOverlapSizeInChars
parameter.description: How many characters of overlap between segments.

function.name: embed
function.return: A Promise resolving to the store

descriptions.description: Embeds a PDF document by splitting it into paragraphs of specified size and overlap, with metadata. The text segments are stored in the embedding store and will get metadata from the pdf and if possible also the name of the PDF is set if that is known. Also additional metadata can be provided that will be attached to each segment.

summaries.summary: Embeds a PDF document by splitting it into paragraphs of specified size and overlap, with metadata.

parameter.name: pdfSource
parameter.description: This can be JSFile or String representing a file path, or byte[] representing the PDF content.

parameter.name: maxSegmentSizeInChars
parameter.description: How many characters per segment we can have.

parameter.name: maxOverlapSizeInChars
parameter.description: How many characters of overlap between segments.

parameter.name: metaData
parameter.description: Metadata to attach to each segment.

function.name: embed
function.return: A Promise resolving to the store

descriptions.description: Asynchronously embeds an array of text data with optional metadata and stores the results.

summaries.summary: Asynchronously embeds an array of text data with optional metadata and stores the results.

parameter.name: data
parameter.description: The array of text strings to embed.

parameter.name: metaData
parameter.description: An array of metadata maps, one for each text string (may be null).

function.name: embedAll
function.return: A Promise resolving with the given foundSet when embeddings are stored, or rejects on error.

descriptions.description: Generates embeddings for all records in the specified foundSet for the given textColumns and stores them in the specified vector column.

summaries.summary: Generates embeddings for all records in the specified foundSet for the given textColumns and stores them in the specified vector column.

parameter.name: foundSet
parameter.description: records in the foundSet are embedded

parameter.name: textColumns
parameter.description: columns of the foundSet to embed

function.name: search
function.return: An array of SearchResult objects representing the best matches.

descriptions.description: Performs a blocking similarity search for the given text, returning the best matches from the store. This returns the default 3 results

summaries.summary: Performs a blocking similarity search for the given text, returning the best matches from the store.

parameter.name: text
parameter.description: The query text to search for.

function.name: search
function.return: An array of SearchResult objects representing the best matches.

descriptions.description: Performs a blocking similarity search for the given text, returning the best matches from the store.

summaries.summary: Performs a blocking similarity search for the given text, returning the best matches from the store.

parameter.name: text
parameter.description: The query text to search for.

parameter.name: maxResults
parameter.description: The maximum number of results to return.

object.description: GeminiChatBuilder is a builder for configuring and creating Gemini chat clients. Allows setting API key, model name, temperature, and memory token limits for the Gemini model.

descriptions.description: Adds a system message to the chat context. This can be a message how the model must behave for this chat session.

summaries.summary: Adds a system message to the chat context.

parameter.name: message
parameter.description: The system message to add.

descriptions.description: Sets the Gemini API key.

summaries.summary: Sets the Gemini API key.

parameter.name: key
parameter.description: The API key.

descriptions.description:
Builds and returns a ChatClient configured with the specified Gemini model settings.

IMPORTANT: When you no longer use the ChatClient returned by .build(), do call .close() on it in order to release resources like MCP server connections or processes.

summaries.summary: Builds and returns a ChatClient configured with the specified Gemini model settings.

function.name: createMCPClient
function.return: a MCPClientBuilder for further configuration; do not forget to call it's .build() method in order to register it with the AI Agent.

descriptions.description:
Create a MCPClientBuilder that can be used to connect to an MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server. Tools exposed by the MCP server to the MCP client (they may be filtered via MCPClientBuilder) will be available to the AI agent.
IMPORTANT: you have to call ChatClient.close(...) when you will no longer use the chat client - in order to clean up the client's resources (including MCP client connections or child processes (in case of STDIO transport/connection))

summaries.summary: Create a MCPClientBuilder that can be used to connect to an MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server.

function.name: createTool
function.return: a ToolBuilder instance for further configuration; call .build on it when you are done configuring the tool in order to register it to the AI Agent.

descriptions.description: Creates a new ToolBuilder for defining a custom tool for the AI agent.

summaries.summary: Creates a new ToolBuilder for defining a custom tool for the AI agent.

parameter.name: toolFunction
parameter.description: the function representing the tool's logic

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: the name of the tool

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: the description of the tool

descriptions.description: Sets the maximum number of memory tokens for chat history. Default this is not set and no memory will be used. So it will only send/use the current prompt and not previous messages.

summaries.summary: Sets the maximum number of memory tokens for chat history.

parameter.name: tokens
parameter.description: The maximum number of tokens.

descriptions.description: Sets the Gemini model name.

summaries.summary: Sets the Gemini model name.

parameter.name: modelName
parameter.description: The model name.

descriptions.description: Sets the temperature for the Gemini model.

summaries.summary: Sets the temperature for the Gemini model.

parameter.name: temperature
parameter.description: The temperature value.

descriptions.description: Injects the built-in Servoy tools (such as user info) into the AI agent.

summaries.summary: Injects the built-in Servoy tools (such as user info) into the AI agent.

parameter.name: useBuiltInTools
parameter.description: Boolean to indicate whether to use built-in tools.

object.description: GeminiEmbeddingModelBuilder is a builder for configuring and creating Gemini embedding models. Allows setting API key and model name for the Gemini embedding model.

descriptions.description: Sets the Gemini API key for the embedding model.

summaries.summary: Sets the Gemini API key for the embedding model.

parameter.name: key
parameter.description: The API key.

descriptions.description: Builds and returns an EmbeddingModel configured with the specified Gemini embedding model settings.

summaries.summary: Builds and returns an EmbeddingModel configured with the specified Gemini embedding model settings.

descriptions.description: Sets the Gemini model name for the embedding model.

summaries.summary: Sets the Gemini model name for the embedding model.

parameter.name: modelName
parameter.description: The model name.

object.description:
This uses the builder pattern to construct & configure connections to MCP servers for the AI Agent.

You are required to configure at least one type of connection via a connectVia...() call in order to be able to call .build(). All other configurations are optional and help configure the interaction with the MCP server, if you want to override the defaults.

Usage example: 

let chatClientBuilder = plugins.ai.createGeminiChatBuilder();
let mcpClient = chatClientBuilder.createMCPClient()
        .connectViaStreamableHTTP("https://myMCPUrl")
        .toolExecutionTimeout(120 * 1000)
        .build();
 let chatClient = chatClientBuilder.build();

descriptions.description:
Enables or disables the automatic health check feature. When enabled, the client will periodically send ping messages to the server to ensure the connection is alive, and will attempt to reconnect if it's not.

The default is enabled

summaries.summary: Enables or disables the automatic health check feature.

parameter.name: autoHealthCheck
parameter.description: {Boolean}

descriptions.description:
Sets the interval for the automatic health checks.
This is only used when the auto health check feature is enabled.

The default is 30 seconds.

summaries.summary: Sets the interval for the automatic health checks.

parameter.name: autoHealthCheckInterval
parameter.description: {Number} the auto health check interval in milliseconds.

descriptions.description:
Create the actual MCP Client based on what was called already on this builder.

IMPORTANT: you have to call ChatClient.close(...) when you will no longer use the chat client - in order to clean up the client's resources (including MCP client connections or child processes (in case of STDIO transport/connection))

summaries.summary: Create the actual MCP Client based on what was called already on this builder.

descriptions.description:
If set to true, the client will cache the resource and resource template lists obtained from the server until it's notified by the server that the resources have changed.
If set to false, there is no caching and the client will always fetch the resource list from the server.

The default is true.

summaries.summary: If set to true, the client will cache the resource and resource template lists obtained from the server until it's notified by the server that the resources have changed.

parameter.name: cacheResourceList
parameter.description: {Boolean}

descriptions.description:
If set to true, the client will cache the tool list obtained from the server until it's notified by the server that the tools have changed or until the cache is evicted.
If set to false, there is no tool caching and the client will always fetch the tool list from the server.

The default is true.

summaries.summary: If set to true, the client will cache the tool list obtained from the server until it's notified by the server that the tools have changed or until the cache is evicted.

parameter.name: cacheToolList
parameter.description: {Boolean}

descriptions.description:
Sets the name that the client will use to identify itself to the MCP server in the initialization message.

The default value is "langchain4j".

summaries.summary: Sets the name that the client will use to identify itself to the MCP server in the initialization message.

parameter.name: clientName
parameter.description: {String}

descriptions.description:
Sets the version string that the client will use to identify itself to the MCP server in the initialization message.

The default value is "1.0".

summaries.summary: Sets the version string that the client will use to identify itself to the MCP server in the initialization message.

parameter.name: clientVersion
parameter.description: {String}

descriptions.description:
Connect to an MCP Server via the STDIO (communication over standard in and standard out). The client will launch an MCP Server as a sub-process and then communicate with it via the in/out streams.

It will not set specific environment variables for the spawned MCP Server sub-process.

summaries.summary: Connect to an MCP Server via the STDIO (communication over standard in and standard out).

parameter.name: command
parameter.description: {Array<String>} the command to be used to start the MCP Server

descriptions.description: Connect to an MCP Server via the STDIO (communication over standard in and standard out). The client will launch an MCP Server as a sub-process and then communicate with it via the in/out streams.

summaries.summary: Connect to an MCP Server via the STDIO (communication over standard in and standard out).

parameter.name: command
parameter.description: {Array<String>} the command to be used to start the MCP Server

parameter.name: environment
parameter.description: {Object<String>} any environment variables that are needed; the key is the variable name and the value is the value of that environment variable

descriptions.description:
Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

the server operates as an independent process (even remotely) that can handle multiple client connections. This transport uses HTTP POST and GET requests. Server can optionally make use of Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream multiple server messages. This permits basic MCP servers, as well as more feature-rich servers supporting streaming and server-to-client notifications and requests.

NOTE: the authentication part (what is required depends on the MCP server) should be prepared beforehand. Use the "headers" param. in order to give the needed access rights to the MCP server (it may be some API key or an OAuth token resulted from using the oauth plugin for example). It does not follow redirects. Subsidiary SSE channel is disabled.

summaries.summary: Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: {String} the URL of the MCP Server to connect to.

descriptions.description:
Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

the server operates as an independent process (even remotely) that can handle multiple client connections. This transport uses HTTP POST and GET requests. Server can optionally make use of Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream multiple server messages. This permits basic MCP servers, as well as more feature-rich servers supporting streaming and server-to-client notifications and requests.

NOTE: the authentication part (what is required depends on the MCP server) should be prepared beforehand. Use the "headers" param. in order to give the needed access rights to the MCP server (it may be some API key or an OAuth token resulted from using the oauth plugin for example). It does not follow redirects. Subsidiary SSE channel is disabled.

summaries.summary: Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: {String} the URL of the MCP Server to connect to.

parameter.name: headers
parameter.description: {Object<String>} any request headers that need to be given to the MCP Server. The object contains String keys that are header names -> the values of those headers.

descriptions.description:
Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

the server operates as an independent process (even remotely) that can handle multiple client connections. This transport uses HTTP POST and GET requests. Server can optionally make use of Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream multiple server messages. This permits basic MCP servers, as well as more feature-rich servers supporting streaming and server-to-client notifications and requests.

NOTE: the authentication part (what is required depends on the MCP server) should be prepared beforehand. Use the "headers" param. in order to give the needed access rights to the MCP server (it may be some API key or an OAuth token resulted from using the oauth plugin for example). It does not follow redirects. Subsidiary SSE channel is disabled.

summaries.summary: Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: {String} the URL of the MCP Server to connect to.

parameter.name: headers
parameter.description: {Object<String>} any request headers that need to be given to the MCP Server. The object contains String keys that are header names -> the values of those headers.

parameter.name: timeoutMillis
parameter.description: {Number} the connection timeout in milliseconds (applied at the http client level). Application-level timeouts are handled by other methods of the MCPClientBuilder itself.

descriptions.description:
Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

the server operates as an independent process (even remotely) that can handle multiple client connections. This transport uses HTTP POST and GET requests. Server can optionally make use of Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream multiple server messages. This permits basic MCP servers, as well as more feature-rich servers supporting streaming and server-to-client notifications and requests.

NOTE: the authentication part (what is required depends on the MCP server) should be prepared beforehand. Use the "headers" param. in order to give the needed access rights to the MCP server (it may be some API key or an OAuth token resulted from using the oauth plugin for example). It does not follow redirects.

summaries.summary: Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: {String} the URL of the MCP Server to connect to.

parameter.name: headers
parameter.description: {Object<String>} any request headers that need to be given to the MCP Server. The object contains String keys that are header names -> the values of those headers.

parameter.name: timeoutMillis
parameter.description: {Number} the connection timeout in milliseconds (applied at the http client level). Application-level timeouts are handled by other methods of the MCPClientBuilder itself.

parameter.name: subsidiaryChannel
parameter.description: {Boolean} enables or disables the subsidiary SSE channel. When enabled, the transport will open an HTTP GET-based SSE stream after initialization, allowing the server to send notifications and requests to the client without the client first sending data via HTTP POST. If the server does not support the subsidiary channel (returns 405), the transport will log a warning and continue without it. If the stream breaks after being successfully established, the transport will automatically attempt to reconnect. Defaults to false.

descriptions.description:
Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

the server operates as an independent process (even remotely) that can handle multiple client connections. This transport uses HTTP POST and GET requests. Server can optionally make use of Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream multiple server messages. This permits basic MCP servers, as well as more feature-rich servers supporting streaming and server-to-client notifications and requests.

NOTE: the authentication part (what is required depends on the MCP server) should be prepared beforehand. Use the "headers" param. in order to give the needed access rights to the MCP server (it may be some API key or an OAuth token resulted from using the oauth plugin for example).

summaries.summary: Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: {String} the URL of the MCP Server to connect to.

parameter.name: headers
parameter.description: {Object<String>} any request headers that need to be given to the MCP Server. The object contains String keys that are header names -> the values of those headers.

parameter.name: timeoutMillis
parameter.description: {Number} the connection timeout in milliseconds (applied at the http client level). Application-level timeouts are handled by other methods of the MCPClientBuilder itself.

parameter.name: subsidiaryChannel
parameter.description: {Boolean} enables or disables the subsidiary SSE channel. When enabled, the transport will open an HTTP GET-based SSE stream after initialization, allowing the server to send notifications and requests to the client without the client first sending data via HTTP POST. If the server does not support the subsidiary channel (returns 405), the transport will log a warning and continue without it. If the stream breaks after being successfully established, the transport will automatically attempt to reconnect. Defaults to false.

parameter.name: followRedirects
parameter.description: {Boolean} enables or disables following HTTP redirects (3xx status codes). When enabled, the transport will automatically follow redirects using HttpClient.Redirect.NORMAL policy (always redirect, except from HTTPS to HTTP). Defaults to false.

descriptions.description:
Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

the server operates as an independent process (even remotely) that can handle multiple client connections. This transport uses HTTP POST and GET requests. Server can optionally make use of Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream multiple server messages. This permits basic MCP servers, as well as more feature-rich servers supporting streaming and server-to-client notifications and requests.

NOTE: the authentication part (what is required depends on the MCP server) should be prepared beforehand. Use the "headers" param. in order to give the needed access rights to the MCP server (it may be some API key or an OAuth token resulted from using the oauth plugin for example).

summaries.summary: Connect to an MCP Server via the StreamableHTTP.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: {String} the URL of the MCP Server to connect to.

parameter.name: headers
parameter.description: {Object<String>} any request headers that need to be given to the MCP Server. The object contains String keys that are header names -> the values of those headers.

parameter.name: timeoutMillis
parameter.description: {Number} the connection timeout in milliseconds (applied at the http client level). Application-level timeouts are handled by other methods of the MCPClientBuilder itself.

parameter.name: subsidiaryChannel
parameter.description: {Boolean} enables or disables the subsidiary SSE channel. When enabled, the transport will open an HTTP GET-based SSE stream after initialization, allowing the server to send notifications and requests to the client without the client first sending data via HTTP POST. If the server does not support the subsidiary channel (returns 405), the transport will log a warning and continue without it. If the stream breaks after being successfully established, the transport will automatically attempt to reconnect. Defaults to false.

parameter.name: followRedirects
parameter.description: {Boolean} enables or disables following HTTP redirects (3xx status codes). When enabled, the transport will automatically follow redirects using HttpClient.Redirect.NORMAL policy (always redirect, except from HTTPS to HTTP). Defaults to false.

parameter.name: forceHTTPVersion1_1
parameter.description: {Boolean} forces the transport to use HTTP/1.1 instead of the default HTTP/2.

descriptions.description:
Sets the timeout for initializing the client.

The default value is 30 seconds.

summaries.summary: Sets the timeout for initializing the client.

parameter.name: initializationTimeout
parameter.description: {Number} the initialization timeout in milliseconds.

descriptions.description:
The timeout to apply when waiting for a ping response. Currently, this is only used in the health check - if the server does not send a pong within this timeframe, the health check will fail.

The default timeout is 10 seconds.

summaries.summary: The timeout to apply when waiting for a ping response.

parameter.name: pingTimeout
parameter.description: {Number} the ping timeout in milliseconds.

descriptions.description:
Sets the protocol version that the client will advertise in the initialization message.

The default value will change over time in newer langchain4j versions. For example: "2024-11-05".

summaries.summary: Sets the protocol version that the client will advertise in the initialization message.

parameter.name: protocolVersion
parameter.description: {String}

descriptions.description: The delay before attempting to reconnect after a failed connection. The default is 5 seconds.

summaries.summary: The delay before attempting to reconnect after a failed connection.

parameter.name: reconnectIntervalMillis
parameter.description: {Number} the reconnect interval in milliseconds.

descriptions.description:
Sets the timeout for resource-related operations (listing resources as well as reading the contents of a resource).

The default value is 60 seconds. A value of zero means no timeout.

summaries.summary: Sets the timeout for resource-related operations (listing resources as well as reading the contents of a resource).

parameter.name: resourcesTimeoutMillis
parameter.description: {Number} the resources timeout in milliseconds.

descriptions.description:
Specify the initial set of roots that are available to the server upon its request.
It is an array of 'root' objects that have the following keys: { name: String, uri: String }

summaries.summary: Specify the initial set of roots that are available to the server upon its request.

parameter.name: roots
parameter.description: {Array<{name: String, uri: String}>} an array of 'root' objects that have the following keys: { name: String, uri: String }

descriptions.description:
Sets the timeout for tool execution. This value applies to each tool execution individually.

The default value is 60 seconds. A value of zero means no timeout.

summaries.summary: Sets the timeout for tool execution.

parameter.name: toolExecutionTimeoutMillis
parameter.description: {Number} the tool execution timeout in milliseconds.

descriptions.description: Sets the error message to return when a tool execution times out. The default value is "There was a timeout executing the tool".

summaries.summary: Sets the error message to return when a tool execution times out.

parameter.name: toolExecutionTimeoutErrorMessage
parameter.description: {String}

descriptions.description: If you do not want all the tools from the MCP Server to be available to the AI Agent, you can provide a list of tool names to expose.

summaries.summary: If you do not want all the tools from the MCP Server to be available to the AI Agent, you can provide a list of tool names to expose.

parameter.name: toolNameFilters
parameter.description: {Array<String>} only tools with the names present in this param will be made available to the AI Agent.

object.description: OpenAiChatBuilder is a builder for configuring and creating OpenAI chat clients. Allows setting API key, model name, temperature, and memory token limits for the OpenAI model.

descriptions.description: Adds a system message to the chat context. This can be a message how the model must behave for this chat session.

summaries.summary: Adds a system message to the chat context.

parameter.name: message
parameter.description: The system message to add.

descriptions.description: Sets the OpenAI API key.

summaries.summary: Sets the OpenAI API key.

parameter.name: key
parameter.description: The API key.

descriptions.description: Sets base url for an api like IONOS that is compatible with OpenAI API.

summaries.summary: Sets base url for an api like IONOS that is compatible with OpenAI API.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: The base URL.

descriptions.description:
Builds and returns a ChatClient configured with the specified OpenAI model settings.

IMPORTANT: When you no longer use the ChatClient returned by .build(), do call .close() on it in order to release resources like MCP server connections or processes.

summaries.summary: Builds and returns a ChatClient configured with the specified OpenAI model settings.

function.name: createMCPClient
function.return: a MCPClientBuilder for further configuration; do not forget to call it's .build() method in order to register it with the AI Agent.

descriptions.description:
Create a MCPClientBuilder that can be used to connect to an MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server. Tools exposed by the MCP server to the MCP client (they may be filtered via MCPClientBuilder) will be available to the AI agent.
IMPORTANT: you have to call ChatClient.close(...) when you will no longer use the chat client - in order to clean up the client's resources (including MCP client connections or child processes (in case of STDIO transport/connection))

summaries.summary: Create a MCPClientBuilder that can be used to connect to an MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server.

function.name: createTool
function.return: a ToolBuilder instance for further configuration; call .build on it when you are done configuring the tool in order to register it to the AI Agent.

descriptions.description: Creates a new ToolBuilder for defining a custom tool for the AI agent.

summaries.summary: Creates a new ToolBuilder for defining a custom tool for the AI agent.

parameter.name: toolFunction
parameter.description: the function representing the tool's logic

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: the name of the tool

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: the description of the tool

descriptions.description: Sets the maximum number of memory tokens for chat history. Default this is not set and no memory will be used. So it will only send/use the current prompt and not previous messages.

summaries.summary: Sets the maximum number of memory tokens for chat history.

parameter.name: tokens
parameter.description: The maximum number of tokens.

descriptions.description: Sets the OpenAI model name.

summaries.summary: Sets the OpenAI model name.

parameter.name: modelName
parameter.description: The model name.

descriptions.description: Sets the temperature for the OpenAI model.

summaries.summary: Sets the temperature for the OpenAI model.

parameter.name: temperature
parameter.description: The temperature value.

descriptions.description: Injects the built-in Servoy tools (such as user info) into the AI agent.

summaries.summary: Injects the built-in Servoy tools (such as user info) into the AI agent.

parameter.name: useBuiltInTools
parameter.description: Boolean to indicate whether to use built-in tools.

object.description: OpenAiEmbeddingModelBuilder is a builder for configuring and creating OpenAI embedding models. Allows setting API key and model name for the OpenAI embedding model.

descriptions.description: Sets the OpenAI API key for the embedding model.

summaries.summary: Sets the OpenAI API key for the embedding model.

parameter.name: key
parameter.description: The API key.

descriptions.description: Sets base url for an api like IONOS that is compatible with OpenAI API.

summaries.summary: Sets base url for an api like IONOS that is compatible with OpenAI API.

parameter.name: url
parameter.description: The base URL.

descriptions.description: Builds and returns an EmbeddingClient configured with the specified OpenAI embedding model settings.

summaries.summary: Builds and returns an EmbeddingClient configured with the specified OpenAI embedding model settings.

descriptions.description: Sets the OpenAI model name for the embedding model.

summaries.summary: Sets the OpenAI model name for the embedding model.

parameter.name: modelName
parameter.description: The model name.

object.description: SearchResult represents a single result from an embedding similarity search. Contains the similarity score, matched text, and associated metadata.

descriptions.description: Returns the metadata associated with this result as a JS Object.

summaries.summary: Returns the metadata associated with this result as a JS Object.

descriptions.description: Returns the similarity score for this result.

summaries.summary: Returns the similarity score for this result.

descriptions.description: Returns the matched text for this result.

summaries.summary: Returns the matched text for this result.

descriptions.description: Sets the addText option (store original text).

summaries.summary: Sets the addText option (store original text).

parameter.name: addText
parameter.description: addText option.

function.name: build
function.return: An EmbeddingStore backed by a servoy store, or null if creation fails.

descriptions.description:
Creates a Servoy embedding store for the specified table name.

The meta data columns are based on the specified meta data columns. Alternatively, when a dataSource is specified, the key columns of that table are used as meta data columns.

summaries.summary: Creates a Servoy embedding store for the specified table name.

descriptions.description: Sets the dataSource to read data from.

summaries.summary: Sets the dataSource to read data from.

parameter.name: dataSource
parameter.description: dataSource.

function.name: metaDataColumn
function.return: A meta data column adder instance.

descriptions.description: Create a meta data column adder for adding a meta data column to this builder instance. Use the `add` method to add the column to this builder instance.

summaries.summary: Create a meta data column adder for adding a meta data column to this builder instance.

descriptions.description: Convenience method to add a TEXT meta data column to this builder. This is equivalent to metaDataColumn().name(name).add()

summaries.summary: Convenience method to add a TEXT meta data column to this builder.

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: meta data column name.

descriptions.description: Sets the recreate option (remove persistent storage) before opening the store.

summaries.summary: Sets the recreate option (remove persistent storage) before opening the store.

parameter.name: recreate
parameter.description: recreate option.

descriptions.description: Sets the serverName to store the embeddings in. Defaults to same server as `dataSource` server.

summaries.summary: Sets the serverName to store the embeddings in.

parameter.name: serverName
parameter.description: serverName.

descriptions.description: Sets the tableName to store the embeddings in.

summaries.summary: Sets the tableName to store the embeddings in.

parameter.name: tableName
parameter.description: tableName.

object.description:
ToolBuilder is a builder class for constructing AI tool specifications with parameter definitions.

This class allows dynamic creation of tool specifications for AI agents, tools that are provided via Servoy functions supporting string, number, and boolean parameters.

Usage example: 

let toolBuilder = plugins.ai.createGeminiChatBuilder().createTool(toolFunction, toolName, toolDescription);
toolBuilder.addStringParameter("param1", "Description", true).addNumberParameter("param2", "Description", false).build();
// ...

descriptions.description: Adds a boolean parameter to the tool specification.

summaries.summary: Adds a boolean parameter to the tool specification.

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: the parameter name

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: the parameter description

parameter.name: required
parameter.description: whether the parameter is required

descriptions.description: Adds a number parameter to the tool specification.

summaries.summary: Adds a number parameter to the tool specification.

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: the parameter name

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: the parameter description

parameter.name: required
parameter.description: whether the parameter is required

descriptions.description: Adds a string parameter to the tool specification.

summaries.summary: Adds a string parameter to the tool specification.

parameter.name: name
parameter.description: the parameter name

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: the parameter description

parameter.name: required
parameter.description: whether the parameter is required

descriptions.description: Finalizes the tool specification and returns the chat builder.

summaries.summary: Finalizes the tool specification and returns the chat builder.

object.description: AIProvider class that provides access to create AI chat and embedding clients/builders.

descriptions.description: Creates a builder for Gemini chat models.

summaries.summary: Creates a builder for Gemini chat models.

descriptions.description: Creates a Gemini chat client using the provided API key and model name. This is a quick way to create a client without using the builder.

summaries.summary: Creates a Gemini chat client using the provided API key and model name.

parameter.name: apiKey
parameter.description: The Gemini API key.

parameter.name: modelName
parameter.description: The Gemini model name.

descriptions.description: Creates a builder for Gemini embeddings.

summaries.summary: Creates a builder for Gemini embeddings.

descriptions.description: Creates an OpenAI chat client using the provided API key and model name. This is a quick way to create a client without using the builder.

summaries.summary: Creates an OpenAI chat client using the provided API key and model name.

parameter.name: apiKey
parameter.description: The OpenAI API key.

parameter.name: modelName
parameter.description: The OpenAI model name.

descriptions.description: Creates a builder for OpenAI chat models.

summaries.summary: Creates a builder for OpenAI chat models.

descriptions.description: Creates a builder for OpenAI store.

summaries.summary: Creates a builder for OpenAI store.

function.name: decodeToonToJson
function.return: JSON string, or null on error

descriptions.description: Decode a TOON formatted string to JSON.

summaries.summary: Decode a TOON formatted string to JSON.

parameter.name: toon
parameter.description: TOON formatted string

function.name: encodeToonFromJson
function.return: TOON formatted string, or null on error

descriptions.description: Encode a JSON string to TOON format.

summaries.summary: Encode a JSON string to TOON format.

parameter.name: json
parameter.description: JSON string to encode
